author: @patrickbrasil7465
text: I didn't know what a diesel punk lamp was and seeing you toss all the gears and syncros to the side like that I was overcome with anxiety and thought how would you remember the orientation of all those parts I was relieved when I realized that there wouldn't be any rotation and wasn't going to be any actual diesel involved😆 anyway super cool lamp I bet loads of people would be willing to buy a decorative lamp like that for their garage, a workshop, or some gear heads mancave.

author: @travismoore7849
text: Still looks better than my lamp made of old clear plastic cups from fast foot joins with a solar led light on top to turn on at night for a  night light. Just a modified hanging solar lamp or that has the led hanging down under the to with a solar panel goes over the clear plastic cups that act to diffuse the light. Sure I put a sub C nicd cell at 1500mah and sure a 1w led. And a small 1000mh coil across the little coil in the circuit to boost the light output. Inductance goes down in parallel. And up in series. So the lower the inductance the more current you can get.

author: @reypolice5231
text:
If you can make a bunch of these, they sell at flea markets for a lot. Like 200 to $500.

If you put your name on it, and made in England on the label one of a kind art piece it'll sell in the US at a flea market for like $500 to some crazy collector.

I'm sure you can find somebody in the US on your list of students that is a flea market aficionado.

You can set up agreements for flea markets to sell on consignment for commission.

One of a kind lamp pieces sell and so do one of a kind clocks, to people with a lot of money that put them in their office to have something to talk about to their friends and clients.

Heck if I were you I would go down to the Canterbury automotive car repair guy and pick through his junk pile and make some clocks and more lamps and sell them at a flea market. Luke would be great for that.

The Young apprentice to the mystery artist always goes over well.

I had three of my art pieces in the Guggenheim museum. Personally I thought they were junk art.

 I won first prize in an art exhibit in three separate categories on three separate pieces of art. 

Was offered a full art scholarship from the 4th grade through to college from the Guggenheim museum, in New York City, for what I considered to be ridiculous art pieces.

So have at it. You're a talented artist more than you know, and people will buy your stuff.

author: @David_Mash
text: Make a loop of copper tube, fill with ferrofluid, heat one end and cool the other. Measure the electrical current!

replies.author: @David_Mash
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering NASA used ferrofluid in pvc pipes with copper patches to creates a fuel pump with no mechanical moving parts. Ferrofluid does not have its own magnetic field so for my experiment you would actually need MR fluid because it does have a magnetic field. I am trying to find a media/fluid to move a magnetic field through a metal using only heating and cooling in order to produce an electric current with no mechanically moving parts.

author: @chuxxsss
text: Well done mate, looks great.

author: @lesclowes6042
text: Nice mate  , but holding the  Ark rod between your first and second finger is a dumb idea, it gets hot fast and reduces your control

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I have been doing it this way for a few months now mate and it works for me where I need a bit more control - so I get it that it doesn't work for you and you maybe have held on too long or tried to do a long weld this way - but for finer work in short bursts with greater control needed it works well as a technique - you should revisit it - but again try not to do too long a weld with this

author: @gazzaka

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: well there are two thoughts on that - one is hide them - the other is don't hide them - either works well - it depends on what you as the designer want to achieve - but exposed cable can be an interesting element of any design
